memberdef.name: PCIE_CFG_CAH_LAT_HD_REG
memberdef.initializer: 0x0003 /* Cache Line/Latency * Timer/Header Type/BIST * Register Offset */

briefdescription.para: This initialize an IP built as an end point.

detaileddescription.para: This function initializes the AXI PCIE end point IP.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to an instance of XAxiPcie data structure represents an end point IP.
is a pointer to an instance of XAxiPcie data structure represents an end point IP.
is AXI PCIe IP unique Device Id

briefdescription.para: This function transfers data from Source Address to Destination Address using the AXI CDMA.

detaileddescription.para: User has to specify the Source Address, Destination Address and Transfer Length in AXICDMA_SRC_ADDR, AXICDMA_DEST_ADDR and AXICDMA_LENGTH defines respectively.

DeviceId
is device ID of the XAxiCdma Device.
XST_SUCCESS if successful
XST_FAILURE.if unsuccessful.
If the hardware system is not built correctly, this function may never return to the caller. User has to specify the Source Address, Destination Address and Transfer Length in AXICDMA_SRC_ADDR, AXICDMA_DEST_ADDR and AXICDMA_LENGTH defines respectively.

parameterdescription.para: is device ID of the XAxiCdma Device.

simplesect.para: If the hardware system is not built correctly this function may never return to the caller.

briefdescription.para: This function is the entry point for AXI PCIe End Point with AXI CDMA Example.
This file contains a design example for using AXI PCIe IP and its driver.

detaileddescription.para: The example handles AXI PCIe IP when it is configured as an end point. It shows how to transfer data between system memory and end point memory. The user has to enter both addresses for source and destination. One of the addresses should be in system memory and the other one in the memory local to an end point(mapped to memory space of the system).

This example assumes that there is an AXI CDMA IP in the system. The user has to specify the Source, Destination and the Length of the DMA transfer which are valid for this system and are defined AXICDMA_SRC_ADDR, AXICDMA_DEST_ADDR and AXICDMA_LENGTH respectively in this example.

This code will illustrate how the AXI Pcie IP and its standalone driver can be used to:
Initialize a PCIe bridge core built as an end point
Retrieve root complex configuration assigned to end point
Move data form system memory to end point memory using AXICDMA IP.

We tried to use as much of the driver's API calls as possible to show the reader how each call could be used and that probably made the example not the shortest way of doing the tasks shown as they could be done.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a rkv 03/07/11 Initial version based on PLB PCIE example 2.00a nm 10/19/11 Renamed function call XAxiPcie_GetRequestId to XAxiPcie_GetRequesterId 3.1 ms 01/23/17 Added xil_printf statement in main function to ensure that "Successfully ran" and "Failed" strings are available in all examples. This is a fix for CR-965028. ms 04/05/17 Added tabspace for return statements in functions for proper documentation while generating doxygen. *
